title: Are AI labs pelicanmaxxing? – Dylan Castillo
excerpt: I generated 1,000+ SVGs across 7 frontier models to test whether AI labs are training on Simon Willison’s pelican-riding-a-bicycle benchmark.
content_html:
For the past few years, Simon Willison has tested every major LLM release with the same prompt: “Generate an SVG of a pelican riding a bicycle”.

What began as a tongue-in-cheek benchmark has become one of the most famous informal benchmarks in AI. Simon’s pelican-on-a-bicycle results are often among the most upvoted comments on Hacker News threads announcing new releases from AI labs.

The benchmark is now famous enough that there’s plenty of discussion about its usefulness and about whether AI labs might be benchmaxxing1 on it. When billions or even trillions of dollars are at stake, and a strong result could help persuade users, wouldn’t it be tempting to pelicanmaxx your model just a bit?

I wanted to find out, so I put together a small experiment. I generated 1,008 SVGs across seven frontier models, scored them with an LLM judge, and used Claude Fable 5 for the analysis.

This article presents the results. All the code is available on Github.

How I tested it

I built a grid of 8 animals × 6 vehicles = 48 prompts, where the famous prompt is one cell:

  1. Animals: pelican, flamingo, heron, otter, raccoon, antelope, whale, cat
  2. Vehicles: bicycle, unicycle, skateboard, scooter, plane, boat

Every prompt uses almost identical phrasing to Simon’s, only switching the animal and vehicle. The animal and vehicle selection wasn’t done in a very rigorous manner, but I tried to vary both similarity to the original prompt and difficulty. Flamingo and heron are quite similar to pelicans; cat, raccoon, and otter are easy cases; antelope is hard; and whale is as different as you can get.

I tested seven models through OpenRouter: GPT-5.6 Terra, Claude Sonnet 5, Gemini 3.5 Flash, Grok 4.5, Qwen3.7-Max, GLM-5.2, and DeepSeek V4 Pro. I generated 3 samples per prompt, at temperature 1.0, requesting the same reasoning effort from every model. That resulted in 1,008 SVGs.

Then I ran each image through a three-stage pipeline:

  1. Rendering: Each SVG is rendered to PNG. If a model returns no SVG or one that fails to render, I regenerate until it produces a valid one, and record the number of attempts. There were only 11 retries across the 1,008 generations.
  2. Judging: GPT-5.6 Luna scores each image with 1-5 ratings for the animal, the vehicle, and the coherence of the action. When I rank animals or vehicles below, I use the matching rating on its own. When I need one number per image, I use the average of the three, which I call the judge score.
  3. Feature extraction: For a more detailed analysis, I also passed each rendered image to Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, which recorded the animal and vehicle it recognized, which way the subject faces, and an open-ended list of scene elements.

My hypothesis is that if a lab trained on the benchmark, it should show up in some combination of the pelican row scoring above what the animal deserves, the bicycle column scoring above what the vehicle deserves, or the specific pelican-bicycle cell beating both.

Evidence #1: The pelicans on bicycles don’t look any better

Before any scoring, the simplest test is to look at the images yourself. Pick a lab to see everything it drew, with the judge’s score under each image (click to open full size):

I looked through the images myself before running the analysis below. Nothing jumped out at me. I couldn’t find a case where the pelican-bicycle images looked noticeably better than the rest of that model’s grid. Maybe in GLM-5.2’s first sample it felt slightly better than the rest, but that batch also produced a pretty cool heron on a skateboard, so I cannot say for sure. Otherwise they look like the rest of what each model draws, and the labs that draw good pelicans on bicycles also do a good job drawing other animal-vehicle combinations.

But this test is hard to replicate, and everyone will have a different opinion. So I wanted something more quantitative, which is why I opted for the method detailed above.

Evidence #2: Labs are not better at drawing pelicans

Here’s the mean animal rating per animal, pooled across all models:

The pelican is 6th of 8, behind cat, whale, raccoon, heron, and antelope. If AI labs were training on the benchmark, you’d expect pelicans at the top. Instead they’re in the bottom half. All seven labs draw cats, whales, and raccoons better than pelicans.

Of course, a pelican may simply be harder to draw than a cat. A lab could train on pelicans and still not push them past the easy animals, so this ranking alone can’t rule that out. I’ll adjust for difficulty in Evidence #4.

Evidence #3: Labs are not better at drawing bicycles

Bicycles fare even worse. They sit second from last, in a near-tie with planes, which come in last:

If labs were training on the benchmark, you’d expect bicycles near the top of this ranking. They’re not. However, the same caveat applies here. A bicycle is harder to draw than a skateboard: it needs two matching wheels, a frame that reaches both axles, handlebars, a seat, and pedals. The judge flags a missing or disconnected one of those on 2/3 of the bicycle images. You can train on bicycle images and still not do a great job relative to simpler vehicles.

One note on the plane, though: I should’ve picked “airplane” instead of “plane” because models often read it geometrically. They drew the animal standing on a flat surface instead of flying an aircraft. The plane is the only vehicle where the feature extractor sometimes found no vehicle at all (25 of 168 images, against zero for the other five), and 20% of plane images scored a 1 or 2 on the vehicle rating, against 5% for bicycles and none at all for boats, scooters, or skateboards.

Evidence #4: Labs are not better at drawing pelicans on bicycles, even adjusting for difficulty

Put the two together and the “pelican on a bicycle” ends up near the bottom of the ranking, at #42 of 48:

But again, some combinations might be just harder to draw than others.

To account for that, I fit a fixed-effects regression on all 1,008 images: score ~ lab + animal × vehicle, plus per-lab interaction terms for pelican, bicycle, and the pelican-bicycle cell, with robust standard errors. The animal × vehicle terms absorb the inherent difficulty of all 48 combinations. The interactions measure each lab’s benchmark-specific boost relative to the average lab, with confidence intervals.

The results:

  1. Every per-lab pelican effect (the lab’s boost on pelicans across all six vehicles) lands between -0.11 and +0.14 judge points, and none comes close to significance (smallest p = 0.25).
  2. The per-lab bicycle effects (the lab’s boost on bicycles across all eight animals) run from Grok 4.5 at -0.18 (p=0.11) to Gemini 3.5 Flash at +0.27 (p=0.022). Only Gemini clears p < 0.05, and the seven point in both directions.
  3. No pelican-bicycle cell effect (the extra boost on the specific combination, on top of the lab’s pelican and bicycle effects) clears p < 0.05. The largest positive is GLM-5.2 at +0.35 (p=0.12), which is the one I mentioned earlier. It’s the closest thing to a signal in this experiment, but still within chance.

Here are the full per-lab estimates. A pelicanmaxxing lab would show dots to the right of the zero line across its whole row:

Every pelican interval and every cell interval contains zero. Exactly one doesn’t: Gemini 3.5 Flash in the bicycle column. But with 21 tests at p < 0.05, chance alone predicts about one false positive (21 × 0.05 ≈ 1.05), and one is exactly what came up. It also doesn’t survive a multiple-comparisons correction: the Bonferroni threshold across the 21 tests is 0.05/21 ≈ 0.002, and its p-value is 0.022. The full table of estimates and p-values is in the repo.

But these intervals are wide, about ±0.6 judge points on average. Any boost smaller than that won’t be captured by this test.

Evidence #5: The pelican-bicycle scenes don’t look memorized

Some have suggested that the pelican on a bicycle looks like a memorized composition, pointing to recurring patterns such as the pelican always facing right, or recurring elements like a sun or a scarf. So I wanted to know if this was true.

Direction: All 21 pelican-bicycle images, across all seven labs, face right. No other animal/vehicle combination does that.

However, facing right is common: 60% of all 1,008 images do it. How common depends on the animal and the vehicle, and bicycles are one of the two vehicles where it’s strongest:

vehicle left right ambiguous
scooter 9% 83% 8%
bicycle 11% 81% 8%
skateboard 19% 60% 21%
plane 21% 58% 21%
unicycle 22% 45% 33%
boat 33% 35% 32%

Pelicans are also among the animals that tend to face right:

animal left right ambiguous
antelope 21% 78% 1%
pelican 22% 78% 0%
heron 22% 77% 1%
whale 34% 65% 1%
flamingo 36% 64% 0%
otter 8% 45% 47%
cat 8% 40% 52%
raccoon 3% 36% 61%

It’s hard to draw a pelican or a bicycle facing the viewer, so models almost always draw them from the side, facing left or right. That’s why so few of their images are ambiguous. Other combinations also come close to unanimous: antelope on a scooter and pelican on a scooter land at 20 of 21, and heron on a bicycle at 19 of 21. So 21 out of 21 doesn’t seem like an outlier.

Scene elements: I let the extractor name any element it saw in the image. These are the counts:

A memorized scene would show up as the same set of elements recurring picture after picture. I went looking for that, and found some combinations do tend to produce the same elements every time. Every single flamingo on a boat has a sun in it. Otters on planes wear scarves 38% of the time. Cats on bicycles get a basket 38% of the time.

The pelican on a bicycle doesn’t seem to have anything particularly different about it. It just has some elements that appear more frequently, like every other animal-vehicle combination.

Limitations

  1. Using a single LLM judge for scoring. Every score here comes from one model, GPT-5.6 Luna, looking at one image at a time. I didn’t do much alignment and didn’t check how often it agrees with itself on a re-run. If a model just can’t judge a drawing reliably, none of the numbers above mean much. The judge is also from the same family as one of the contestants, GPT-5.6 Terra. However, every lab draws all 48 combinations, so a judge that happens to like one lab’s style lifts that lab’s whole grid at once. But that doesn’t change the results because this analysis only cares about the within-lab differences.
  2. SVGmaxxing. A lab that optimized SVG generation as a whole (or a subset such as animals on vehicles) rises on every cell at once and looks identical to a lab that’s just good. Some labs, such as Google/DeepMind, openly do this. This experiment can’t detect that.
  3. Limited budget. The whole experiment ran on roughly $80 of API credits. That capped it at 3 samples per cell, a single judge, and 7 models. This also prevented me from iterating too much on the prompts and pipeline, as with the “plane” vs. “airplane” case.

Conclusion

Sorry, HN haters, but there’s little evidence that AI labs are pelicanmaxxing. Or at least they’re not doing it in a plainly obvious manner.

Pelicans aren’t drawn any better than other animals. Bicycles aren’t drawn any better than other vehicles. And no lab draws the combination better than its pelicans and bicycles already predict. GLM-5.2 comes closest: it has the largest boost on the exact pelican-bicycle cell, and its first pelican-on-bicycle sample caught my eye. But the effect is small and not significant, so I wouldn’t put too much weight on it.

The other thing that stands out is direction in the scene composition. All 21 pelican-bicycle images face right, the only combination in the grid where every image agrees. But it doesn’t seem that strange. Facing right is the norm across the experiment. Three other combinations land at 90% or above, and with 48 of them, I’m not surprised one reached 21 out of 21.

The more plausible story is SVGmaxxing like Google/DeepMind does. Other labs might be doing it more quietly. Sadly, this experiment can’t say who’s doing it. But at least you can sleep tonight knowing that AI labs are not producing terabytes of pelicans on bicycles just to trick Simon Willison.

If you want to look at the data yourself, the full pipeline is in the repo.

Footnotes

  1. the practice of optimizing AI models to achieve high scores on popular benchmarks.↩︎

blocks.html_en: Every prompt uses almost identical phrasing to Simon’s, only switching the animal and vehicle. The animal and vehicle selection wasn’t done in a very rigorous manner, but I tried to vary both similarity to the original prompt and difficulty. Flamingo and heron are quite similar to pelicans; cat, raccoon, and otter are easy cases; antelope is hard; and whale is as different as you can get.

sentences.en: Every prompt uses almost identical phrasing to Simon’s, only switching the animal and vehicle.
sentences.zh: 每个提示都使用与西蒙几乎相同的措辞，只是交换了动物和车辆。

sentences.en: The animal and vehicle selection wasn’t done in a very rigorous manner, but I tried to vary both similarity to the original prompt and difficulty.
sentences.zh: 动物和车辆的选择并不是以非常严格的方式完成的，但我尝试改变与原始提示和难度的相似性。

sentences.en: Flamingo and heron are quite similar to pelicans; cat, raccoon, and otter are easy cases; antelope is hard; and whale is as different as you can get.
sentences.zh: 火烈鸟和苍鹭与鹈鹕非常相似；猫、浣熊和水獭是简单的例子；羚羊是硬的；而鲸鱼则与你所能得到的完全不同。

blocks.html_en: I tested seven models through OpenRouter: GPT-5.6 Terra, Claude Sonnet 5, Gemini 3.5 Flash, Grok 4.5, Qwen3.7-Max, GLM-5.2, and DeepSeek V4 Pro. I generated 3 samples per prompt, at temperature 1.0, requesting the same reasoning effort from every model. That resulted in 1,008 SVGs.

sentences.en: I tested seven models through OpenRouter: GPT-5.6 Terra, Claude Sonnet 5, Gemini 3.5 Flash, Grok 4.5, Qwen3.7-Max, GLM-5.2, and DeepSeek V4 Pro.
sentences.zh: 我通过 OpenRouter 测试了七个模型：GPT-5.6 Terra、Claude Sonnet 5、Gemini 3.5 Flash、Grok 4.5、Qwen3.7-Max、GLM-5.2 和 DeepSeek V4 Pro。

sentences.en: I generated 3 samples per prompt, at temperature 1.0, requesting the same reasoning effort from every model.
sentences.zh: 我在温度 1.0 下为每个提示生成 3 个样本，要求每个模型进行相同的推理工作。

sentences.en: That resulted in 1,008 SVGs.
sentences.zh: 最终生成了 1,008 个 SVG。

blocks.html_en: Then I ran each image through a three-stage pipeline:

sentences.en: Then I ran each image through a three-stage pipeline:
sentences.zh: 然后我通过三阶段管道运行每个图像：

blocks.html_en: • Rendering: Each SVG is rendered to PNG. If a model returns no SVG or one that fails to render, I regenerate until it produces a valid one, and record the number of attempts. There were only 11 retries across the 1,008 generations.

sentences.en: Rendering: Each SVG is rendered to PNG.
sentences.zh: 渲染：每个 SVG 都会渲染为 PNG。

sentences.en: If a model returns no SVG or one that fails to render, I regenerate until it produces a valid one, and record the number of attempts.
sentences.zh: 如果模型没有返回 SVG 或无法渲染，我会重新生成，直到生成有效的 SVG，并记录尝试次数。

sentences.en: There were only 11 retries across the 1,008 generations.
sentences.zh: 1,008 代中仅重试了 11 次。

blocks.html_en: • Judging: GPT-5.6 Luna scores each image with 1-5 ratings for the animal, the vehicle, and the coherence of the action. When I rank animals or vehicles below, I use the matching rating on its own. When I need one number per image, I use the average of the three, which I call the judge score.

sentences.en: Judging: GPT-5.6 Luna scores each image with 1-5 ratings for the animal, the vehicle, and the coherence of the action.
sentences.zh: 评审：GPT-5.6 Luna 对每张图像的动物、车辆和动作的连贯性进行 1-5 分评分。

sentences.en: When I rank animals or vehicles below, I use the matching rating on its own.
sentences.zh: 当我对下面的动物或车辆进行排名时，我会单独使用匹配的评级。

sentences.en: When I need one number per image, I use the average of the three, which I call the judge score.
sentences.zh: 当我需要每张图像一个数字时，我会使用三个数字的平均值，我称之为评判分数。

blocks.html_en: • Feature extraction: For a more detailed analysis, I also passed each rendered image to Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, which recorded the animal and vehicle it recognized, which way the subject faces, and an open-ended list of scene elements.

sentences.en: Feature extraction: For a more detailed analysis, I also passed each rendered image to Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, which recorded the animal and vehicle it recognized, which way the subject faces, and an open-ended list of scene elements.
sentences.zh: 特征提取：为了进行更详细的分析，我还将每个渲染图像传递给 Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite，它记录了它识别的动物和车辆、拍摄对象面向的方向以及场景元素的开放式列表。

blocks.html_en: My hypothesis is that if a lab trained on the benchmark, it should show up in some combination of the pelican row scoring above what the animal deserves, the bicycle column scoring above what the vehicle deserves, or the specific pelican-bicycle cell beating both.

sentences.en: My hypothesis is that if a lab trained on the benchmark, it should show up in some combination of the pelican row scoring above what the animal deserves, the bicycle column scoring above what the vehicle deserves, or the specific pelican-bicycle cell beating both.
sentences.zh: 我的假设是，如果一个实验室接受了基准测试，它应该以某种组合的形式出现：鹈鹕行得分高于动物应有的得分，自行车列得分高于车辆应得的得分，或者特定的鹈鹕自行车细胞击败了两者。

blocks.html_en: Evidence #1: The pelicans on bicycles don’t look any better

sentences.en: Evidence #1: The pelicans on bicycles don’t look any better
sentences.zh: 证据#1：骑自行车的鹈鹕看起来也好不到哪儿去

blocks.html_en: I looked through the images myself before running the analysis below. Nothing jumped out at me. I couldn’t find a case where the pelican-bicycle images looked noticeably better than the rest of that model’s grid. Maybe in GLM-5.2’s first sample it felt slightly better than the rest, but that batch also produced a pretty cool heron on a skateboard, so I cannot say for sure. Otherwise they look like the rest of what each model draws, and the labs that draw good pelicans on bicycles also do a good job drawing other animal-vehicle combinations.

sentences.en: Otherwise they look like the rest of what each model draws, and the labs that draw good pelicans on bicycles also do a good job drawing other animal-vehicle combinations.

blocks.html_en: But this test is hard to replicate, and everyone will have a different opinion. So I wanted something more quantitative, which is why I opted for the method detailed above.

sentences.en: But this test is hard to replicate, and everyone will have a different opinion.

sentences.zh: 所以我想要更定量的东西，这就是为什么我选择上面详述的方法。

blocks.html_en: The pelican is 6th of 8, behind cat, whale, raccoon, heron, and antelope. If AI labs were training on the benchmark, you’d expect pelicans at the top. Instead they’re in the bottom half. All seven labs draw cats, whales, and raccoons better than pelicans.

sentences.zh: 鹈鹕在八种动物中排名第六，排在猫、鲸鱼、浣熊、苍鹭和羚羊之后。

sentences.en: If AI labs were training on the benchmark, you’d expect pelicans at the top.

blocks.html_en: Of course, a pelican may simply be harder to draw than a cat. A lab could train on pelicans and still not push them past the easy animals, so this ranking alone can’t rule that out. I’ll adjust for difficulty in Evidence #4.

sentences.en: Evidence #3: Labs are not better at drawing bicycles

blocks.html_en: Bicycles fare even worse. They sit second from last, in a near-tie with planes, which come in last:

sentences.en: Bicycles fare even worse.

blocks.html_en: If labs were training on the benchmark, you’d expect bicycles near the top of this ranking. They’re not. However, the same caveat applies here. A bicycle is harder to draw than a skateboard: it needs two matching wheels, a frame that reaches both axles, handlebars, a seat, and pedals. The judge flags a missing or disconnected one of those on 2/3 of the bicycle images. You can train on bicycle images and still not do a great job relative to simpler vehicles.

sentences.en: If labs were training on the benchmark, you’d expect bicycles near the top of this ranking.

sentences.en: They’re not.
sentences.zh: 他们不是。

sentences.en: However, the same caveat applies here.
sentences.zh: 然而，同样的警告也适用于此。

sentences.en: A bicycle is harder to draw than a skateboard: it needs two matching wheels, a frame that reaches both axles, handlebars, a seat, and pedals.
sentences.zh: 自行车比滑板更难画：它需要两个匹配的轮子、一个到达两个车轴的框架、车把、一个座位和踏板。

blocks.html_en: One note on the plane, though: I should’ve picked “airplane” instead of “plane” because models often read it geometrically. They drew the animal standing on a flat surface instead of flying an aircraft. The plane is the only vehicle where the feature extractor sometimes found no vehicle at all (25 of 168 images, against zero for the other five), and 20% of plane images scored a 1 or 2 on the vehicle rating, against 5% for bicycles and none at all for boats, scooters, or skateboards.

sentences.en: One note on the plane, though: I should’ve picked “airplane” instead of “plane” because models often read it geometrically.
sentences.zh: 不过，关于飞机的一个注释是：我应该选择“飞机”而不是“飞机”，因为模型经常从几何角度来解读它。

sentences.en: They drew the animal standing on a flat surface instead of flying an aircraft.
sentences.zh: 他们画的是站在平坦表面上的动物，而不是驾驶飞机。

sentences.en: The plane is the only vehicle where the feature extractor sometimes found no vehicle at all (25 of 168 images, against zero for the other five), and 20% of plane images scored a 1 or 2 on the vehicle rating, against 5% for bicycles and none at all for boats, scooters, or skateboards.
sentences.zh: 飞机是唯一一种特征提取器有时根本找不到任何车辆的车辆（168 张图像中的 25 张，其他 5 张图像为零），20% 的平面图像在车辆评级上得分为 1 或 2，而自行车的这一比例为 5%，而船、踏板车或滑板则完全没有。

sentences.zh: 证据#4：实验室并不擅长画自行车上的鹈鹕，甚至根据难度进行调整

sentences.en: Put the two together and the “pelican on a bicycle” ends up near the bottom of the ranking, at #42 of 48:

sentences.en: But again, some combinations might be just harder to draw than others.

blocks.html_en: To account for that, I fit a fixed-effects regression on all 1,008 images: score ~ lab + animal × vehicle, plus per-lab interaction terms for pelican, bicycle, and the pelican-bicycle cell, with robust standard errors. The animal × vehicle terms absorb the inherent difficulty of all 48 combinations. The interactions measure each lab’s benchmark-specific boost relative to the average lab, with confidence intervals.

sentences.en: To account for that, I fit a fixed-effects regression on all 1,008 images: score ~ lab + animal × vehicle, plus per-lab interaction terms for pelican, bicycle, and the pelican-bicycle cell, with robust standard errors.
sentences.zh: 为了解释这一点，我对所有 1,008 张图像进行了固定效应回归：得分 ~ 实验室 + 动物 × 车辆，加上鹈鹕、自行车和鹈鹕-自行车细胞的每个实验室交互项，并具有稳健的标准误差。

sentences.en: The animal × vehicle terms absorb the inherent difficulty of all 48 combinations.
sentences.zh: 动物 × 车辆术语吸收了所有 48 种组合的固有难度。

sentences.en: The interactions measure each lab’s benchmark-specific boost relative to the average lab, with confidence intervals.
sentences.zh: 这些交互作用通过置信区间衡量每个实验室相对于平均实验室的基准特定提升。

sentences.en: The results:
sentences.zh: 结果：

blocks.html_en: • Every per-lab pelican effect (the lab’s boost on pelicans across all six vehicles) lands between -0.11 and +0.14 judge points, and none comes close to significance (smallest p = 0.25).

sentences.en: Every per-lab pelican effect (the lab’s boost on pelicans across all six vehicles) lands between -0.11 and +0.14 judge points, and none comes close to significance (smallest p = 0.25).
sentences.zh: 每个实验室的鹈鹕效应（实验室对所有六辆车上的鹈鹕的增强）都落在 -0.11 到 +0.14 判断点之间，没有一个接近显着性（最小 p = 0.25）。

blocks.html_en: • The per-lab bicycle effects (the lab’s boost on bicycles across all eight animals) run from Grok 4.5 at -0.18 (p=0.11) to Gemini 3.5 Flash at +0.27 (p=0.022). Only Gemini clears p < 0.05, and the seven point in both directions.

sentences.en: The per-lab bicycle effects (the lab’s boost on bicycles across all eight animals) run from Grok 4.5 at -0.18 (p=0.11) to Gemini 3.5 Flash at +0.27 (p=0.022).
sentences.zh: 每个实验室的自行车效应（实验室对所有八只动物的自行车的促进作用）从 Grok 4.5 (-0.18 (p=0.11)) 到 Gemini 3.5 Flash (+0.27 (p=0.022))。

sentences.en: Only Gemini clears p < 0.05, and the seven point in both directions.
sentences.zh: 只有双子座明确 p < 0.05，并且 7 指向两个方向。

blocks.html_en: • No pelican-bicycle cell effect (the extra boost on the specific combination, on top of the lab’s pelican and bicycle effects) clears p < 0.05. The largest positive is GLM-5.2 at +0.35 (p=0.12), which is the one I mentioned earlier. It’s the closest thing to a signal in this experiment, but still within chance.

sentences.en: No pelican-bicycle cell effect (the extra boost on the specific combination, on top of the lab’s pelican and bicycle effects) clears p < 0.05.
sentences.zh: 没有鹈鹕-自行车细胞效应（除了实验室的鹈鹕和自行车效应之外，特定组合的额外增强）清除 p < 0.05。

sentences.en: The largest positive is GLM-5.2 at +0.35 (p=0.12), which is the one I mentioned earlier.
sentences.zh: 最大的正数是 GLM-5.2，为 +0.35 (p=0.12)，这就是我之前提到的。

sentences.en: It’s the closest thing to a signal in this experiment, but still within chance.
sentences.zh: 这是本实验中最接近信号的东西，但仍有机会。

blocks.html_en: Here are the full per-lab estimates. A pelicanmaxxing lab would show dots to the right of the zero line across its whole row:

sentences.en: Here are the full per-lab estimates.
sentences.zh: 以下是每个实验室的完整估计。

sentences.en: A pelicanmaxxing lab would show dots to the right of the zero line across its whole row:
sentences.zh: pelicanmaxxing 实验室会在整行零线右侧显示点：

blocks.html_en: Every pelican interval and every cell interval contains zero. Exactly one doesn’t: Gemini 3.5 Flash in the bicycle column. But with 21 tests at p < 0.05, chance alone predicts about one false positive (21 × 0.05 ≈ 1.05), and one is exactly what came up. It also doesn’t survive a multiple-comparisons correction: the Bonferroni threshold across the 21 tests is 0.05/21 ≈ 0.002, and its p-value is 0.022. The full table of estimates and p-values is in the repo.

sentences.en: Every pelican interval and every cell interval contains zero.
sentences.zh: 每个鹈鹕区间和每个单元区间都包含零。

sentences.en: Exactly one doesn’t: Gemini 3.5 Flash in the bicycle column.
sentences.zh: 恰恰有一个没有：自行车栏中的 Gemini 3.5 Flash。

sentences.en: But with 21 tests at p < 0.05, chance alone predicts about one false positive (21 × 0.05 ≈ 1.05), and one is exactly what came up.
sentences.zh: 但是，在 p < 0.05 的 21 次测试中，仅凭机会就预测出大约 1 个误报 (21 × 0.05 ≈ 1.05)，而其中一个正是出现的结果。

sentences.en: It also doesn’t survive a multiple-comparisons correction: the Bonferroni threshold across the 21 tests is 0.05/21 ≈ 0.002, and its p-value is 0.022.
sentences.zh: 它也无法通过多重比较校正：21 个测试的 Bonferroni 阈值是 0.05/21 ≈ 0.002，其 p 值为 0.022。

sentences.en: The full table of estimates and p-values is in the repo.
sentences.zh: 完整的估计值和 p 值表位于存储库中。

blocks.html_en: But these intervals are wide, about ±0.6 judge points on average. Any boost smaller than that won’t be captured by this test.

sentences.en: But these intervals are wide, about ±0.6 judge points on average.
sentences.zh: 但这些区间很宽，平均约为±0.6个判断点。

sentences.en: Any boost smaller than that won’t be captured by this test.
sentences.zh: 此测试不会捕获任何小于该值的提升。

blocks.html_en: Evidence #5: The pelican-bicycle scenes don’t look memorized

sentences.en: Evidence #5: The pelican-bicycle scenes don’t look memorized
sentences.zh: 证据#5：鹈鹕自行车的场景看起来没有被记住

blocks.html_en: Some have suggested that the pelican on a bicycle looks like a memorized composition, pointing to recurring patterns such as the pelican always facing right, or recurring elements like a sun or a scarf. So I wanted to know if this was true.

sentences.en: Some have suggested that the pelican on a bicycle looks like a memorized composition, pointing to recurring patterns such as the pelican always facing right, or recurring elements like a sun or a scarf.

blocks.html_en: It’s hard to draw a pelican or a bicycle facing the viewer, so models almost always draw them from the side, facing left or right. That’s why so few of their images are ambiguous. Other combinations also come close to unanimous: antelope on a scooter and pelican on a scooter land at 20 of 21, and heron on a bicycle at 19 of 21. So 21 out of 21 doesn’t seem like an outlier.

Cats on bicycles get a basket 38% of the time.

blocks.html_en: • Using a single LLM judge for scoring. Every score here comes from one model, GPT-5.6 Luna, looking at one image at a time. I didn’t do much alignment and didn’t check how often it agrees with itself on a re-run. If a model just can’t judge a drawing reliably, none of the numbers above mean much. The judge is also from the same family as one of the contestants, GPT-5.6 Terra. However, every lab draws all 48 combinations, so a judge that happens to like one lab’s style lifts that lab’s whole grid at once. But that doesn’t change the results because this analysis only cares about the within-lab differences.

blocks.html_en: • SVGmaxxing. A lab that optimized SVG generation as a whole (or a subset such as animals on vehicles) rises on every cell at once and looks identical to a lab that’s just good. Some labs, such as Google/DeepMind, openly do this. This experiment can’t detect that.

blocks.html_en: • Limited budget. The whole experiment ran on roughly $80 of API credits. That capped it at 3 samples per cell, a single judge, and 7 models. This also prevented me from iterating too much on the prompts and pipeline, as with the “plane” vs. “airplane” case.

blocks.html_en: Sorry, HN haters, but there’s little evidence that AI labs are pelicanmaxxing. Or at least they’re not doing it in a plainly obvious manner.

blocks.html_en: Pelicans aren’t drawn any better than other animals. Bicycles aren’t drawn any better than other vehicles. And no lab draws the combination better than its pelicans and bicycles already predict. GLM-5.2 comes closest: it has the largest boost on the exact pelican-bicycle cell, and its first pelican-on-bicycle sample caught my eye. But the effect is small and not significant, so I wouldn’t put too much weight on it.

content_html_zh:
在过去的几年里，Simon Willison 测试了每个主要的法学硕士版本，并给出了相同的提示：“生成骑自行车的鹈鹕的 SVG”。

最初只是一个半开玩笑的基准测试，现已成为人工智能领域最著名的非正式基准测试之一。 西蒙的鹈鹕骑自行车的结果通常是宣布人工智能实验室新版本的黑客新闻帖子中获得最多支持的评论之一。

该基准现在已经足够出名，以至于有很多关于它的实用性以及人工智能实验室是否可以在其上进行基准测试的讨论。 当数十亿甚至数万亿美元处于危险之中，并且强有力的结果可以帮助说服用户时，稍微对你的模型进行 pelicanmaxx 不是很诱人吗？

我想找出答案，所以我做了一个小实验。 我在七个前沿模型中生成了 1,008 个 SVG，由法学硕士法官对它们进行评分，并使用 Claude Fable 5 进行分析。

本文介绍了结果。 所有代码都可以在 Github 上找到。

我是如何测试的

我构建了一个由 8 只动物 × 6 辆车 = 48 个提示组成的网格，其中著名的提示是一个单元格：

  1. 动物：鹈鹕、火烈鸟、苍鹭、水獭、浣熊、羚羊、鲸鱼、猫
  2. 交通工具：自行车、独轮车、滑板、踏板车、飞机、船

每个提示都使用与西蒙几乎相同的措辞，只是交换了动物和车辆。 动物和车辆的选择并不是以非常严格的方式完成的，但我尝试改变与原始提示和难度的相似性。 火烈鸟和苍鹭与鹈鹕非常相似；猫、浣熊和水獭是简单的例子；羚羊是硬的；而鲸鱼则与你所能得到的完全不同。

我通过 OpenRouter 测试了七个模型：GPT-5.6 Terra、Claude Sonnet 5、Gemini 3.5 Flash、Grok 4.5、Qwen3.7-Max、GLM-5.2 和 DeepSeek V4 Pro。 我在温度 1.0 下为每个提示生成 3 个样本，要求每个模型进行相同的推理工作。 最终生成了 1,008 个 SVG。

然后我通过三阶段管道运行每个图像：

  1. 渲染：每个 SVG 都会渲染为 PNG。 如果模型没有返回 SVG 或无法渲染，我会重新生成，直到生成有效的 SVG，并记录尝试次数。 1,008 代中仅重试了 11 次。
  2. 评审：GPT-5.6 Luna 对每张图像的动物、车辆和动作的连贯性进行 1-5 分评分。 当我对下面的动物或车辆进行排名时，我会单独使用匹配的评级。 当我需要每张图像一个数字时，我会使用三个数字的平均值，我称之为评判分数。
  3. 特征提取：为了进行更详细的分析，我还将每个渲染图像传递给 Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite，它记录了它识别的动物和车辆、拍摄对象面向的方向以及场景元素的开放式列表。

我的假设是，如果一个实验室接受了基准测试，它应该以某种组合的形式出现：鹈鹕行得分高于动物应有的得分，自行车列得分高于车辆应得的得分，或者特定的鹈鹕自行车细胞击败了两者。

证据#1：骑自行车的鹈鹕看起来也好不到哪儿去

在进行任何评分之前，最简单的测试就是亲自查看图像。 选择一个实验室来查看它绘制的所有内容，每张图像下方都有评委的分数（点击打开全尺寸）：

在进行下面的分析之前，我自己查看了这些图像。 没有任何事情让我惊讶。 我找不到鹈鹕自行车图像看起来明显比该模型网格的其余部分更好的情况。 也许在 GLM-5.2 的第一个样品中，感觉比其他样品稍好一些，但是该批次还生产了一只非常酷的滑板苍鹭，所以我不能肯定地说。 除此之外，它们看起来就像每个模型绘制的其余部分一样，绘制优秀的骑自行车鹈鹕的实验室在绘制其他动物与车辆的组合方面也做得很好。

但这个测试很难复制，每个人都会有不同的看法。 所以我想要更定量的东西，这就是为什么我选择上面详述的方法。

证据#2：实验室并不擅长画鹈鹕

以下是所有模型中每只动物的平均动物评级：

鹈鹕在八种动物中排名第六，排在猫、鲸鱼、浣熊、苍鹭和羚羊之后。 如果人工智能实验室按照基准进行训练，你会期望鹈鹕处于领先地位。 相反，他们位于下半部分。 所有七个实验室都比鹈鹕更能吸引猫、鲸鱼和浣熊。

当然，鹈鹕可能比猫更难画。 实验室可以对鹈鹕进行训练，但仍然无法将它们超越简单的动物，因此仅靠这个排名并不能排除这种可能性。 我将根据证据 #4 的难度进行调整。

证据#3：实验室并不擅长绘制自行车

自行车的情况更糟。 他们排在倒数第二位，与排在最后的飞机几乎并列：

如果实验室按照基准进行培训，您会期望自行车接近该排名的顶部。 他们不是。 然而，同样的警告也适用于此。 自行车比滑板更难画：它需要两个匹配的轮子、一个到达两个车轴的框架、车把、一个座位和踏板。 法官在 2/3 的自行车图像上标记出其中一张丢失或断开。 你可以在自行车图像上进行训练，但相对于更简单的车辆来说仍然做得不够好。

不过，关于飞机的一个注释是：我应该选择“飞机”而不是“飞机”，因为模型经常从几何角度来解读它。 他们画的是站在平坦表面上的动物，而不是驾驶飞机。 飞机是唯一一种特征提取器有时根本找不到任何车辆的车辆（168 张图像中的 25 张，其他 5 张图像为零），20% 的平面图像在车辆评级上得分为 1 或 2，而自行车的这一比例为 5%，而船、踏板车或滑板则完全没有。

证据#4：实验室并不擅长画自行车上的鹈鹕，甚至根据难度进行调整

将两者放在一起，“自行车上的鹈鹕”最终排名垫底，在 48 项中排名第 42：

但同样，某些组合可能比其他组合更难绘制。

为了解释这一点，我对所有 1,008 张图像进行了固定效应回归：得分 ~ 实验室 + 动物 × 车辆，加上鹈鹕、自行车和鹈鹕-自行车细胞的每个实验室交互项，并具有稳健的标准误差。 动物 × 车辆术语吸收了所有 48 种组合的固有难度。 这些交互作用通过置信区间衡量每个实验室相对于平均实验室的基准特定提升。

结果：

  1. 每个实验室的鹈鹕效应（实验室对所有六辆车上的鹈鹕的增强）都落在 -0.11 到 +0.14 判断点之间，没有一个接近显着性（最小 p = 0.25）。
  2. 每个实验室的自行车效应（实验室对所有八只动物的自行车的促进作用）从 Grok 4.5 (-0.18 (p=0.11)) 到 Gemini 3.5 Flash (+0.27 (p=0.022))。 只有双子座明确 p < 0.05，并且 7 指向两个方向。
  3. 没有鹈鹕-自行车细胞效应（除了实验室的鹈鹕和自行车效应之外，特定组合的额外增强）清除 p < 0.05。 最大的正数是 GLM-5.2，为 +0.35 (p=0.12)，这就是我之前提到的。 这是本实验中最接近信号的东西，但仍有机会。

以下是每个实验室的完整估计。 pelicanmaxxing 实验室会在整行零线右侧显示点：

每个鹈鹕区间和每个单元区间都包含零。 恰恰有一个没有：自行车栏中的 Gemini 3.5 Flash。 但是，在 p < 0.05 的 21 次测试中，仅凭机会就预测出大约 1 个误报 (21 × 0.05 ≈ 1.05)，而其中一个正是出现的结果。 它也无法通过多重比较校正：21 个测试的 Bonferroni 阈值是 0.05/21 ≈ 0.002，其 p 值为 0.022。 完整的估计值和 p 值表位于存储库中。

但这些区间很宽，平均约为±0.6个判断点。 此测试不会捕获任何小于该值的提升。

证据#5：鹈鹕自行车的场景看起来没有被记住

一些人认为自行车上的鹈鹕看起来像是记忆中的构图，指出了重复出现的图案，例如鹈鹕总是面向右侧，或者重复出现的元素，例如太阳或围巾。 所以我想知道这是否属实。

方向：所有 7 个实验室的所有 21 张鹈鹕自行车图像都面向右侧。 没有其他动物/车辆组合可以做到这一点。

然而，面向右侧的情况很常见：1,008 张图像中，有 60% 的人都这样做。 常见程度取决于动物和车辆，自行车是其最强的两种车辆之一：

车辆 left right 模糊的
小型摩托车 9% 83% 8%
自行车 11% 81% 8%
滑板 19% 60% 21%
plane 21% 58% 21%
独轮车 22% 45% 33%
boat 33% 35% 32%

鹈鹕也是倾向于面朝右侧的动物之一：

动物 left right 模糊的
羚羊 21% 78% 1%
鹈 22% 78% 0%
heron 22% 77% 1%
whale 34% 65% 1%
火烈鸟 36% 64% 0%
otter 8% 45% 47%
cat 8% 40% 52%
狸 3% 36% 61%

很难画出面向观众的鹈鹕或自行车，因此模特几乎总是从侧面画它们，面向左或面向右。 这就是为什么他们的图像很少有模棱两可的原因。 其他组合也接近一致：骑踏板车的羚羊和骑踏板车的鹈鹕在 21 个中排名第 20，骑自行车的苍鹭在 21 个中排名第 19。 因此，21 个中的 21 个似乎并不是异常值。

场景元素：我让提取器命名它在图像中看到的任何元素。 这些是计数：

记忆的场景将显示为同一组元素，一张又一张地重复出现。 我去寻找这一点，发现一些组合确实每次都会产生相同的元素。 船上的每只火烈鸟身上都有一个太阳。 飞机上的水獭 38% 的时间都戴着围巾。 骑自行车的猫有 38% 的机会得到篮子。

骑自行车的鹈鹕似乎没有什么特别不同的地方。 它只是有一些出现更频繁的元素，就像所有其他动物与车辆的组合一样。

局限性

  1. 使用单一的法学硕士评委进行评分。 这里的每一个分数都来自一个模型，GPT-5.6 Luna，一次查看一张图像。 我没有做太多调整，也没有检查重新运行时它与自身一致的频率。 如果模型无法可靠地判断图纸，那么上面的数字就没有多大意义。 评委也与参赛者之一GPT-5.6 Terra来自同一个家族。 然而，每个实验室都会抽取全部 48 种组合，因此碰巧喜欢某个实验室风格的评委会立即提升该实验室的整个网格。 但这并不会改变结果，因为该分析只关心实验室内的差异。
  2. SVGmax 。 优化 SVG 生成整体（或子集，例如车辆上的动物）的实验室会立即在每个单元上崛起，并且看起来与刚刚好的实验室相同。 一些实验室，例如 Google/DeepMind，公开这样做。 这个实验无法检测到这一点。
  3. 预算有限。 整个实验运行大约需要 80 美元的 API 积分。 每个单元的上限为 3 个样本、一名法官和 7 个模型。 这也防止我在提示和管道上进行过多的迭代，就像“飞机”与“飞机”的情况一样。

结论

抱歉，讨厌 HN 的人，但几乎没有证据表明人工智能实验室是鹈鹕最大化。 或者至少他们没有以一种显而易见的方式来做这件事。

鹈鹕的画法并不比其他动物好多少。 自行车并不比其他车辆更好。 没有哪个实验室得出的这种组合比鹈鹕和自行车已经预测的更好。 GLM-5.2 最接近：它对鹈鹕自行车电池具有最大的提升，并且它的第一个鹈鹕自行车样本引起了我的注意。 但效果很小，也不显着，所以我不会太重视它。

另一件突出的事情是场景构图的方向。 所有 21 张鹈鹕自行车图像都面向右侧，这是网格中唯一每张图像都一致的组合。 但这似乎并没有那么奇怪。 面向右侧是整个实验的常态。 其他三个组合的得分达到 90% 或以上，其中 48 个组合中，其中一个组合达到 21 个中的 21 个，我对此并不感到惊讶。

更合理的故事是 SVGmaxxing，就像 Google/DeepMind 所做的那样。 其他实验室可能会更安静地进行这项工作。 遗憾的是，这个实验无法说明是谁在做的。 但至少你今晚可以睡觉了，因为你知道人工智能实验室不会仅仅为了欺骗西蒙·威利森（Simon Willison）而生产出数兆兆字节的骑自行车的鹈鹕。
